questions.question:
How do Perseus's responses to individuals and events drive the action of the plot forward? 


Respond to this question in the form of a paragraph that includes a claim, one piece of evidence, and an explanation.

responses.content: Perseus wanted to kill Medusa, he when to these special people that can help him where to go and how to fight Medusa. The first person who told Perseus where to go was Athene. Athene was the person to tell a lot of information on where.To go, who you should talk to  get closer to Medusa, and  she also got him weapons to kill Medusa. The second  person to help Perseus killing Medusa was Hermes, Hermes help Perseus to get this flying thing where you can fly and fly up high. And I’m not talking about wings, I’m talking about sandals that can make Perseus fly to escape Medusa.
responses.bucket_rationale: The response describes what Perseus does and mentions Athene and Hermes helping him, but it does not explain HOW Perseus's responses drive the plot forward. The answer summarizes actions and helpers but never analyzes the cause-effect relationship or mechanism by which Perseus's choices move the story along. There is no explicit claim about how his responses drive the plot, no specific evidence from the text, and no explanation connecting actions to plot movement. This matches Score 1, Pattern A: Primary Unanswered (describes what Perseus does but never explains how this drives the plot forward).

responses.content: So we know that Perseus killed Medusa using powers right? And he want to some people to help him fight Medusa. The first who helped him to kill Medusa was Athene. Athene was the person who helped Perseus to getting closer to kill Medusa. She gave him weapons to defeat Medusa and she told him where to go to talk to the next person who will help him. She told him to talk hermis which gave him these flying sandals so he can go to medusa and fly then walk really up high. After hermis gave him these flying sandals, hermis told him to go to the three gray sisters to help him. But Perseus force them to help him where to go and how to go to Medusa.he took there eye and teeth, they where sobbing and begging so badly for there stuff back and the  only way that they can have there eye AND teeth is that they tell him how to get to Medusa. After they told him everything, he gave them there eye and teeth back.
responses.bucket_rationale: The response describes what Perseus does and who helps him, but it does not explain how Perseus's responses drive the plot forward. The answer summarizes actions and events but does not analyze the cause-effect relationship or explain the mechanism by which Perseus's choices move the story along. There is no explicit reasoning about how his actions create consequences that advance the plot. This matches Score 1, Pattern A: Primary Unanswered—describes what Perseus does but never explains how these actions drive the plot forward.
